dialogues.text: The tearing of the cedulas marked the beginning of open rebellion. The Katipunan, under Bonifacio's leadership, launched attacks against Spanish garrisons.
We fight not for conquest, but for liberation! For our rights, for our homeland! Let every blow struck be a testament to our will!
The forces in Cavite are strong, Supremo. We have had successes, but the Spanish are regrouping.
We must coordinate our efforts. The revolution must be a unified force, not scattered pockets of resistance. Every victory, no matter how small, fuels the spirit of our people.
Bonifacio, as the Supremo of the Katipunan, worked tirelessly to unite the various revolutionary groups, facing internal challenges and the constant pressure of the Spanish military.

scenes.name: Internal Strife and the Tejeros Convention

dialogues.text: As the revolution gained momentum, internal divisions began to surface. The Tejeros Convention was called to establish a formal government, but it led to a painful schism.
Bonifacio is not a lawyer. He is not fit to be Director of the Interior. A lawyer should hold this position!
I was elected by the people! This convention was meant to unite us, not to divide us based on titles!
Despite his efforts, Bonifacio's authority was undermined, leading to his controversial arrest and subsequent execution.
Accused of treason and sedition by factions within the revolutionary government, Bonifacio faced a swift and unjust trial.
I have fought for our freedom. I have given my life to this cause. If this is how my service is repaid, then history will judge.
(He is executed, a tragic end to a pivotal leader.)
